title: Chouchou Refuses to Abandon the Shop
description: On pages 1–4, Chouchou’s loyalty to the pet food shop turns Buggy’s occupation from a pirate threat into a personal wound for the town.
scene: Chouchou refuses to give up the place connected to his owner, while pirates and bystanders react to the dog’s stubborn defense.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I'm feeling FAINT JUST watching THIS!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Get'M, Zolo!!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I can't lose, not even once...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: TO ANYONE WHO CALLS HIMSELF A SWORDSMAN!!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: So you injured yourself as insurance for your reputation... well, don't worry...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: YOUR WOUNDS ARE SEVERE. THEY'LL MAKE AN EXCELLENT EXCUSE WHEN YOU LOSE.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Read this way

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: It's the other way around!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: If I lose to the likes of you when my wounds are only this light...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Then I may as well give up my dream right now.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You scurvy dog!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Hey!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Huh?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: THAT SHACK BEHIND THE RUNS OF THE TAVERN...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: That's where they keep their treasure.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: And Buggy's probably got the map of the Grand Line.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: I'll get their treasure and make my escape.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I'VE GOT TO DO IT NOW, WHILE THEY'RE ALL KNOCKED OUT...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: WHETHER YOU GUYS WIN OR LOSE THIS BATTLE...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I DON'T REALLY CARE!

summary: This part makes the conflict emotional: Luffy sees that Buggy’s violence is not abstract, but aimed at things people love and protect.
